title: St. Marys Church Meenangadi
tagline: St. Marys Church Meenangadi — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: St. Marys Church Meenangadi is a much-loved spiritual town in Kerala, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: E. A. E St. Mary's Soonoro Syriac Orthodox Church, Meenangadi (Malayalam: സെന്‍റ് മേരീസ് 'സൂനോറോ' തീര്‍ത്ഥാടന കേന്ദ്രം മീനങ്ങാടി), is a Marian Pilgrim center of the Syriac Orthodox Church located at Meenangadi in Kerala, India. The church is under E.A.E Arch Diocese, (Malayalam: പൗരസ്ത്യ സുവിശേഷ സമാജം) the first missionary association of Syriac Orthodox Patriarchate of Antioch and All the East, and is currently under the direct control of Ignatius Aphrem II, Patriarch of the Syriac Orthodox Church. topPlaces.name: Meenangadi
topPlaces.description: Meenangadi is an old town situated on the highway NH 766 between Kalpetta and Sulthan Bathery in Wayanad District, in Kerala, India. This place is one among the Provinces where the existence of Dolmens provide historical evidence of earlier civilisation.

topPlaces.name: Krishnagiri Village
topPlaces.description: Krishnagiri is a village in the Wayanad District of Kerala, India. It is in a rural area of Wayanad and one of the 15 villages in Sultan Bathery Tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Cochin Refineries Limited Ground
topPlaces.description: Cochin Refineries Limited Ground or CRL Ground is a multi purpose stadium in Ambalamugal, Kochi, Kerala. The ground is mainly used for organizing matches of football, cricket and other sports.

topPlaces.name: Krishnagiri Stadium
topPlaces.description: Krishnagiri Stadium is a cricket stadium in Wayanad, Kerala. The stadium is located in Krishnagiri Village in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Karapuzha Dam
topPlaces.description: Karapuzha Dam located in the Wayanad district of Kerala, Karapuzha Dam is located in the greenish and natural regions of Wayanad, Kerala on the Karapuzha River, a tributary of the Kabini River. Construction on the dam began in 1977 and it was completed in 2004.

topPlaces.name: Phantom Rock
topPlaces.description: Phantom Rock is a tourist destination in Ambalavayal in the district of Wayanad in Kerala. It is a natural metamorphic rock formation resembling a human skull, and thus is known as Phantom Rock.

topPlaces.name: WMO English Academy, Karunyapuram
topPlaces.description: WMO English Academy, Karunyapuram under Wayanad Muslim Orphanage is an educational institution located in Muttil village at Wayanad district Headquarters Kalpetta.It was the First Center Board of Secondary Education-Certified English school in Wayanad. This school is a member of Wayanad Sahodaya Schools Complex.

topPlaces.name: Palamangalam
topPlaces.description: Palamangalam is a village panchayat in Narayanavanam mandal, located in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. In the 2011 census it had a population of 3754 in 1015 households.

items.note: Meenangadi is an old town situated on the highway NH 766 between Kalpetta and Sulthan Bathery in Wayanad District, in Kerala, India. This place is one among the Provinces where the existence of Dolmens provide historical evidence of earlier civilisation.
Krishnagiri is a village in the Wayanad District of Kerala, India. It is in a rural area of Wayanad and one of the 15 villages in Sultan Bathery Tehsil.
Cochin Refineries Limited Ground or CRL Ground is a multi purpose stadium in Ambalamugal, Kochi, Kerala. The ground is mainly used for organizing matches of football, cricket and other sports.
Krishnagiri Stadium is a cricket stadium in Wayanad, Kerala. The stadium is located in Krishnagiri Village in the Indian state of Kerala.

Karapuzha Dam located in the Wayanad district of Kerala, Karapuzha Dam is located in the greenish and natural regions of Wayanad, Kerala on the Karapuzha River, a tributary of the Kabini River. Construction on the dam began in 1977 and it was completed in 2004.
Phantom Rock is a tourist destination in Ambalavayal in the district of Wayanad in Kerala. It is a natural metamorphic rock formation resembling a human skull, and thus is known as Phantom Rock.

This school is a member of Wayanad Sahodaya Schools Complex.
Palamangalam is a village panchayat in Narayanavanam mandal, located in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. In the 2011 census it had a population of 3754 in 1015 households.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Marys Church Meenangadi?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Marys Church Meenangadi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Marys Church Meenangadi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (63 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (89 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Marys Church Meenangadi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Marys Church Meenangadi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Marys Church Meenangadi?
faq.a: Stays in St. Marys Church Meenangadi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Marys Church Meenangadi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Meenangadi, Krishnagiri Village, Cochin Refineries Limited Ground, Krishnagiri Stadium, Karapuzha Dam.
